description_long: This is a vintage Sony boombox stereo sound system with a bold red exterior and a rectangular, boxy shape. The front features two large black speaker grilles on either side, a central cassette tape deck with a transparent window, and a variety of tactile controls including sliders, buttons, and switches. The top has a prominent integrated handle for portability, while the sides and back are mostly solid red with minimal detailing. The control panel is densely packed with labeled sliders and buttons, and the cassette deck is flanked by tuning and volume controls. The overall design is robust, with a mix of plastic and metal elements, and the object exudes a retro, urban aesthetic suitable for street or park environments.
description: A retro red Sony boombox with dual speakers, a central cassette deck, and a dense array of tactile controls. It features a sturdy handle and a bold, urban design.
description_view_0: Front view showing dual black speaker grilles, cassette deck, and a full array of sliders, buttons, and switches.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the solid red side panel and the depth of the boombox, with minimal visible features.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain red back with two rectangular vents and the handle's recess.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the solid red side and the handle's profile.
attribute_brand_logo: The word 'SONY' is visible in white text on the front panel above the cassette deck and on both speaker grilles.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grilles are black with a circular mesh pattern, featuring a concentric ring design and a central circular accent.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is located on the front, right of the cassette deck, and includes vertical sliders, rectangular buttons, and a round knob, all clearly labeled and arranged in rows.
